# pheno-config

## Purpose
Layered configuration cascade substrate for the pheno-* fleet. Wraps `figment` to overlay IntelliJ run-config, env vars, and TOML files on top of compile-time defaults. 12-factor priority order: `jetbrains > env > toml > default`.

## When to use
- Building a new service in the fleet that needs typed config
- Need a single, fleet-wide priority order for dev / CI / runtime overrides
- Want to read both `.idea/runConfigurations/*.xml` and `PHENO_*` env vars
- Cascading several config sources into one `Figment`

## When NOT to use
- Pure secret storage (use a vault like `HashiCorp Vault` or `phenotype-vault`)
- Hot-reloading runtime config (use a config server like `phenotype-configd`)
- Non-Phenotype projects — the priority order is opinionated

## Quickstart
```rust
use pheno_config::cascade::build_cascade;
let cfg = build_cascade();
let port: u16 = cfg.find_value("server.port")?;
let level: String = cfg.find_value("logging.level")?;
```

## Env vars
- `PHENO_*` — any env var with the `PHENO_` prefix is merged into the cascade (e.g. `PHENO_SERVER_PORT=9090`).

## Key types
- `DEFAULT_TOML` — embedded compile-time defaults (server / logging / database sections)
- `build_cascade() -> Figment` — standard 4-provider cascade (defaults → toml-file → env → jetbrains)
- `build_cascade_from_str(toml: &str) -> Figment` — same cascade with explicit TOML instead of `config.toml`

## Example
See `pheno-config/tests/cascade_test.rs` for the 4 priority tests (default-only, toml-overrides-default, env-overrides-toml, env-only key).
